task: Fix named pipe lifecycle — pipe vanishes between client disconnects on Windows (ERROR_FILE_NOT_FOUND), causing 'workflow timeout' and desktop shell reconnect failure
summary:
Chairman's full analysis led to root cause:

The Windows named pipe server used 16 workers, ALL with
first_pipe_instance(false). On Windows, a named pipe
exists as long as at least one instance is open. When a
client disconnects and its serve_rpc_connection returns,
the worker loops back and calls connect() to wait for
the next client. But between the old instance's disconnect
and the new instance's connect(), the pipe can become
empty — no instance is open — and the next client trying
to connect gets ERROR_FILE_NOT_FOUND (os error 2).

This manifests as:
- workflow runs fine (8 phases complete)
- runtime exits between workflows (pipe vanishes)
- desktop shell tries to reconnect → os error 2
- webview shows 'workflow timeout' even though everything
  finished successfully

Fix: change the first worker's first_pipe_instance from
false to true. On Windows, first_pipe_instance(true)
ensures this instance is always a 'first' instance that
creates the pipe path. The pipe is guaranteed to have at
least one instance open (the first worker) even when all
other workers are between serve/loop-back cycles.

Both rpc_listener (16 workers) and events_listener
(4 workers) get the same fix.

FILES:
  crates/pipe-server/src/server.rs (+23 lines, -8 deletions)
reason: Chairman: '真正需要修的不是 Workflow，而是 Runtime 生命周期管理（Named Pipe Server 生命周期）'. Full analysis shows workflow completes fine — the real bug is the Windows named pipe lifecycle between client disconnects. This is the root cause of the 'workflow timeout' error the chairman saw.
